prompt: Complete the dialogue using the correct present perfect continuous form of the verbs in brackets. Use short forms where possible.
explanation: The present perfect continuous (have/has + been + verb-ing) describes actions continuing up to now, such as studying, working and planning over the past weeks.
passageTemplate:
Mandy: Hi Matt. How are you? What {{b1}} (you / do) recently?
Matt: Oh, hi Mandy! Well, {{b2}} (I / study) for my exams.
Mandy: That sounds boring! {{b3}} (you / work) hard?
Matt: Very! Basically, {{b4}} (I / just / sit) at my desk in my bedroom for the past three weeks and {{b5}} (I / not / go) out at all. {{b6}} (I / work) with Michael, my best friend, some of the time, though, so at least I've had some company. How about you?
Mandy: Well, my mum and {{b7}} (I / paint) my bedroom for the last few days. That has been fun! And {{b8}} (we / also / plan) our summer holiday.
Matt: Great! Where are you going?
Mandy: Well, we haven't decided yet. {{b9}} (We / look) at different places to see which we like best.
Matt: I'm sure you'll have a great time, wherever you go. Oh, by the way, {{b10}} (I / think) of having a party when I finish my exams. Would you like to come?
Mandy: Sure! That would be great!
